{"key_event":"Constitutional gains amid guerrilla-paramilitary expansion","reasoning":"The 1991 Constitution and new rights institutions strengthened legal mobilization and accountability, but rural civil society was badly eroded by kidnapping, massacres, displacement, and narco-paramilitary intimidation. State capacity retained important central, technocratic, and anti-cartel capabilities, yet its monopoly of force and legitimacy declined with Proceso 8000, guerrilla battlefield gains, AUC growth, and the Caguán demilitarized zone.","society_change":-0.3,"society_power":5.2,"state_change":-0.6,"state_power":5.7}
